summary: Blazing Scarlet>Fire and tight second‑set jam from '79 Indy show
blurb: The second set erupts with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, followed by powerful Estimated Prophet, Eyes of the World, The Other One and Wharf Rat. Jerry delivers scorching solos while Phil drops bass bombs, and the rhythm section drives seamless transitions. An audience tape captures the energy cleanly, making it the go‑to source.
review: On February 3, 1979 at Indianapolis’ Market Square Arena the Dead delivered a high‑octane second set that still shines in the archives. The night bursts open with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, propelling into an inspired Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World stretch and a seamless Drums‑Space‑The Other One transition that rolls into Wharf Rat, a jubilant I Need A Miracle and a rousing Casey Jones finale. Jerry Garcia was unmistakably on fire, laying down scorching lead lines and vocal intensity; Phil Lesh dropped bass bombs that drove every jam forward, while Donna Jean’s harmonies added depth. The twin‑drum engine of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lifted each transition, and Keith Godchaux’s piano cut through the mix even when the arena reverberation was harsh. First‑set highlights include a crisp China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider pairing that showcases the band’s tight interplay.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the Scarlet>Fire sequence, China>Rider and second‑set jams, with no consistent negative comments. Positive language such as 'astonishing', 'blistering', 'scorching' appears across several sources, warranting a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with excellent clarity, minimal muddiness, and superb capture of the second‑set surge—including Scarlet>Fire, Estimated Prophet, Eyes, The Other One and Wharf Rat—making it the most complete and listenable version.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain ignites the second set.
Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World jam showcases tight rhythm and Phil’s “bomb‑dropping” bass.
The Other One → Wharf Rat stretch delivers deep improvisational flow.
Audience recording (set 2) offers crystal‑clear sound that rivals a soundboard.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia was on fire, delivering scorching lead solos and vocal intensity throughout, especially during Scarlet Begonias → Fire and the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid low‑end foundation, dropping bass bombs that propelled the jams and standing out in the mix on several recordings.
band_performance.Bob: Weir offered crisp rhythm‑guitar work and steady vocal harmonies early in the set; his vocals sounded dulled only on the poorly mixed SBD capture.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s powerful drumming lifted every transition, driving the energy through Drums‑Space‑The Other One and beyond.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s tight, propulsive drumming complemented Hart, together forming a rhythm engine that kept the second set surging.
